b:Te4f,<div class="question">
<p>How many signals are observed in the <sup>1</sup>H NMR spectrum of this compound?<br/><img height="72" src="data:image/png;base64," style="display:block;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;" width="141"/><br/>A.  1</p>
<p>B.  2</p>
<p>C.  3</p>
<p>D.  4</p>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/chemistry/topic/11/","children":["← back to ","Chemistry"," topic ","11"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.1.SL.TZ2.28"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Chemistry"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","1"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","11-3-spectroscopic-identification-of-organic-compounds"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>A</p>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>The compound used for this <sup>1</sup>H NMR question was unfamiliar to the candidates and it was a skeletal formula. They were expected to notice its symmetry and that all H’s had the same chemical environment. The majority of candidates saw only part of this symmetry and decided on two chemical environments. 29% of the candidates obtained the correct answer. Some teachers commented on the difficulty of this question.</p>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
